---
title: "Numpad keys pass through (numpad-mouse compatible) — plan/report"
author: "Yun Jiang"
date: "2026-07-22"
format:
  html:
    html-math-method: mathjax
include-in-header:
  text: |
    <link rel="icon" href="data:image/svg+xml,<svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 100 100'><text y='.9em' font-size='90'>🖱️</text></svg>">
---

# Goal

With the overlay up, numpad arrow keys (NumLock off) were swallowed as label pan,
breaking the user's AutoHotkey numpad-mouse script (`*NumpadUp`/`*NumpadClear`/
`*NumpadLeft`/`*NumpadRight`). Numpad digit/operator keys were already not captured.

# Root cause

With NumLock off, the numpad nav keys reuse the arrow VK codes (numpad 6 → `VK_RIGHT`,
8 → `VK_UP`, 4 → `VK_LEFT`; 5 → `VK_CLEAR`). The overlay's `Classify` maps the arrow VK
codes to Nudge unconditionally, and the low-level hook sits above AutoHotkey's, so the
key was swallowed before AHK saw it.

# Fix

The `WH_KEYBOARD_LL` extended-key flag (`LLKHF_EXTENDED`) reliably distinguishes the
**dedicated arrow cluster** (extended → still pans labels) from the **numpad keys** (not
extended → pass through). Per the Win32 extended-key list ([Keyboard Input Overview](
https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/win32/inputdev/about-keyboard-input)), extended
keys are "the Arrow keys in the clusters to the left of the numeric keypad" plus only
NumpadDivide/NumpadEnter on the keypad — numpad digit/nav keys are NOT extended.

- `NativeMethods/User32.cs`: added `LLKHF_EXTENDED = 0x0001` (keyboard struct flags).
- `OverlayKeyboardHook.cs`: read `extended = (k.flags & LLKHF_EXTENDED) != 0`; pass it to
  `Classify`. Added a 4-arg `Classify` whose arrow branches only nudge when `extended`;
  the 3-arg overload (tests) defaults `extended = true`.
- Tests: `NumpadArrows_NotExtended_PassThrough` (4 arrows, `extended:false` → `None`).

# Verification

CI compile + xUnit green (existing arrow tests still nudge via the 3-arg default; new
test asserts numpad arrows pass through). Runtime (Windows): with the overlay up and
NumLock off, numpad arrows move the mouse via the AHK script; real arrow keys still pan.

# Note

AutoHotkey's own `NumpadRight` vs `Right` key names are further evidence Windows can tell
them apart — via this same extended flag.
